message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-3vif2 (sigma/omega no-fallback tests)

Fixed in commit cd9a76c. Resolves 9 pre-existing test failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3dxfn","title":"parity: all_pairs_dijkstra_path / all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length outer-key iteration order doesn't match nx","description":"fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path and fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length yield outer-key (source) iteration in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['a','e','c','d','b']) instead of nx's node-insertion order (['c','d','a','b','e']). Drop-in code that iterates 'for source, dists in result.items(): ...' breaks. Same iteration-order family fixed for triangles/core_number/etc. Note: outer keys for all_pairs_shortest_path[_length] already match nx; only dijkstra variants need the fix.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:35:36.535013167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.955576532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.953597595Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 40d5417. all_pairs_dijkstra_path / _length outer keys now iterate in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3lqi6","title":"DiGraph.reverse() / fnx.reverse() edge + adj iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.reverse() (and DiGraph.reverse() method) on a DiGraph returns a copy whose edge-iteration order and per-node adj-iteration order differ from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]  # path
  fnx.reverse(dg).edges() -> [('c','b'),('d','c'),('b','a')]
  nx.reverse(dgx).edges() -> [('b','a'),('c','b'),('d','c')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]  # cycle
  fnx -> [(1,0),(3,2),(0,3),(2,1)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(1,0),(2,1),(3,2)]

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a')]
  fnx adj['a'] = ['d', 'c']
  nx adj['a']  = ['c', 'd']

nx iterates nodes in insertion order and emits reversed (v→u) edges in that order; fnx's Rust-side reverse traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

The copy=False path matches. The copy=True path (default for both function and method) drifts.

Fix: delegate the copy=True path to nx so the resulting DiGraph's edge + adj iteration matches nx's contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:56:39.907591704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.487970050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.485884732Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3qopf","title":"k_truss subgraph node order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.k_truss builds the resulting subgraph from Rust output which iterates nodes in canonical/sort order, while nx iterates them in input-graph node order.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx.k_truss(g, 2).nodes() -> ['a','b','c','d','e']
  nx.k_truss(gx, 2).nodes()  -> ['c','d','a','b','e']

Drop-in code that iterated R.nodes() / R.edges() / R.adj of the k-truss subgraph in nx's input-graph-order broke. Fix: delegate k_truss to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity + _from_nx_graph for output rehydration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:05.403750981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:39:25.048950577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:39:25.046943262Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-3vif2","title":"TestSigmaParity / TestOmegaParity tests assert no-fallback but br-smallrng delegates","description":"9 pre-existing test failures in test_graph_metrics.py:
- TestSigmaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path4, cycle4, complete4]
- TestSigmaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[disconnected4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[complete4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path3, path4, cycle4, disconnected4]

The tests assert that fnx.sigma / fnx.omega compute the result WITHOUT delegating to nx (monkeypatched nx.sigma / nx.omega raises AssertionError if called). But br-smallrng made these wrappers delegate to nx — fnx's local loop with per-iteration derived seeds produced different numeric results than nx's stateful single-RNG approach, so the project chose delegation for exact parity.

Tests are stale — they assert the pre-br-smallrng contract.

Fix: drop test_matches_networkx_without_fallback entirely for sigma/omega (they now legitimately delegate), and remove the error_contract cases that hit the delegation path (only the cases that hit fnx's pre-checks survive).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:15:37.570006310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:16:32.525291845Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-3vif2","title":"TestSigmaParity / TestOmegaParity tests assert no-fallback but br-smallrng delegates","description":"9 pre-existing test failures in test_graph_metrics.py:
- TestSigmaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path4, cycle4, complete4]
- TestSigmaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[disconnected4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[complete4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path3, path4, cycle4, disconnected4]

The tests assert that fnx.sigma / fnx.omega compute the result WITHOUT delegating to nx (monkeypatched nx.sigma / nx.omega raises AssertionError if called). But br-smallrng made these wrappers delegate to nx — fnx's local loop with per-iteration derived seeds produced different numeric results than nx's stateful single-RNG approach, so the project chose delegation for exact parity.

Tests are stale — they assert the pre-br-smallrng contract.

Fix: drop test_matches_networkx_without_fallback entirely for sigma/omega (they now legitimately delegate), and remove the error_contract cases that hit the delegation path (only the cases that hit fnx's pre-checks survive).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:15:37.570006310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:18:46.978893456Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:18:46.976905629Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4d97w","title":"_from_nx_graph (les_miserables / Davis / hoffman_singleton) drifts adj order from nx","description":"fnx's _from_nx_graph converter walks nx.G.edges() in nx's edge-iteration order and feeds them to add_edges_from. Because nx.G.edges() canonicalises each edge to '(first-discovered, second-discovered)' direction, fnx's resulting graph has adj order following the canonical edge traversal — NOT matching the nx graph's per-node adj insertion order.

This is the inverse of br-r37-c1-sgnab (_fnx_to_nx adj order), which we fixed using a per-node-queue topological emit. Same fix applies here in reverse direction.

Repro (les_miserables_graph delegates via _from_nx_graph):
  nx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Labarre','MmeMagloire','MlleBaptistine','Myriel','Marguerite','MmeDeR']
  fnx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Myriel','MlleBaptistine','MmeMagloire','Labarre','Marguerite','MmeDeR']

Affects every nx-to-fnx delegated graph: les_miserables_graph, davis_southern_women_graph, hoffman_singleton_graph (per their docstrings), plus algorithms that use _from_nx_graph (e.g. union, intersection, transitive_reduction, transitive_closure_dag, etc.).

Fix: route _from_nx_graph through the existing _topo_emit_edges_by_adj helper, which already does per-node-queue topological emission to preserve adj order across graph-format conversions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:11:40.761421100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.441351537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.439301764Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4elbw","title":"parity: 4 more functions raise NotImplemented/NetworkXError with custom messages instead of nx's standard wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf — 4 more functions diverge from nx's exact error messages: (1) condensation on undirected raises 'strongly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) flow_hierarchy on undirected raises NetworkXError('flow_hierarchy requires a DiGraph'); nx raises NetworkXError('G must be a digraph in flow_hierarchy'). (3) in_degree_centrality and (4) out_degree_centrality on undirected raise custom 'is not defined for undirected graphs.' messages; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code matching the exact text breaks. Fix: align all four to nx's exact wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:28:50.121233008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.902270771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.900331589Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 35bb5d3. strongly_connected_components/number_strongly/kosaraju/condensation/in_degree_centrality/out_degree_centrality/flow_hierarchy now raise with nx's exact error messages. 17 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-53cwv","title":"kosaraju_strongly_connected_components emission order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components emits SCCs in reverse order vs nx (similar to br-r37-c1-2vdtt for strongly_connected_components, but for the Kosaraju variant).

Repro:
  edges = [(a,b),(a,c),(b,d),(c,d),(d,e)] (DAG)
  fnx -> [{a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}]
  nx  -> [{e}, {d}, {c}, {b}, {a}]

  edges = [(a,b),(b,a),(c,d),(d,c),(a,c)] (two SCCs + bridge)
  fnx -> [{a,b}, {c,d}]
  nx  -> [{c,d}, {a,b}]

Drop-in code that iterates SCCs in nx's Kosaraju-completion order broke. Fix: delegate kosaraju_strongly_connected_components to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the existing strongly_connected_components fix).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:24:32.967954855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:27:23.894212013Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:27:23.892198670Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
